main_subtitle: Detailed answers about principles, ethics, and standard usage
search_placeholder: Search for a question or keyword...
q_certification_answer: No. TCP/UP is a declarative self-labeling protocol. It allows an author to make their AI usage visible, but it is neither third-party certification, technical proof, nor legal validation.
q_detection_title: Is TCP/UP a technical AI detection tool?
q_detection_answer: No. TCP/UP is a voluntary declaration protocol. Unlike detection tools, TCP/UP relies on author declaration and their responsibility to their audience. It is an intellectual honesty approach that establishes a trust relationship with the reader.
q_origine_title: What is the origin of this initiative?
q_origine_answer: This project was designed by an IT professional with 25 years of experience managing information systems. It is a non-profit civic initiative aimed at proposing a universal transparency standard in the face of growing generative AI. (To learn more about the author, see the "About" page.)
q_opposition_title: Does the protocol oppose AI usage?
q_opposition_answer: Absolutely not. The protocol views AI as a major assistance lever. Its purpose is not to judge tool usage, but to make visible the role distribution between human intention and algorithmic assistance.
q_nontextuel_title: Can I use TCP/UP for non-text content (audio, video)?
q_nontextuel_answer: Yes. The protocol was designed for all digital content types: text, image, audio, video, code. The distinction between Substance (intention, structure) and Form (technical execution) applies regardless of media. For complex works, prefer modular labeling or the most representative label.
q_professionnels_answer: No, it is designed for everyone, from amateur bloggers to large enterprises. The simplicity of the decision tree (30 seconds) and free resources make it accessible to all content creators.
cat_pratique_title: Practical Application: Choosing the Right Label
q_correction_title: I used AI to correct spelling or syntax. Which label applies?
q_correction_answer: If AI was not involved at all, the HUC label (no AI used) applies. If it only corrected spelling or grammar without changing substance or style, the HCA label (Human‑Centric Assistance) is most appropriate. For more substantial rephrasing (fluidity improvement, tone change), move to HCE (Human‑Centric Editing).
q_traduction_title: I translated my text with an automated tool (DeepL, ChatGPT). Which label to choose?
q_traduction_answer:
HCE is generally the most appropriate label if your ideas and structure are human and AI intervenes to produce the final version in another language.
If automated translation significantly influences final meaning or AI shapes reformulation beyond simple linguistic transposition, ACE may be more accurate.
q_plan_title: I asked AI to structure my outline before writing. Which label to choose?
q_plan_answer: Once AI intervenes in thought architecture or argument hierarchy, we enter co-construction phase. The ACE label (AI‑Centric Editing) is then required to reflect this collaboration on substance.
q_megaprompt_title: How do I label text generated from very specific instructions (Mega‑Prompt)?
q_megaprompt_answer: HCE can be retained if your instructions already contain the complete intellectual architecture of content. When in doubt, or if AI shaped final text structure, ACE remains the most transparent choice.
q_code_title: I use AI to generate code (or a function), then correct and optimize it. Which label applies?
q_code_answer: In programming, logic (Substance) and syntax (Form) are often inseparable. As soon as AI produces business logic, ACE label is recommended, even after human corrections. If you rewrite the entire code, you can consider HCE, but for transparency, ACE remains the default.
q_image_title: An AI-generated image that I touched up: which label to choose?
q_image_answer: AI-generated image defaults to AIC label (mostly or entirely AI-generated). If you significantly retouch it (composition change, element addition, subject modification), you can move to ACE. Simple color correction or cropping generally doesn't justify a label change.
q_composite_title: My content combines multiple methods (HCE text, AIC image, ACE code). What global label?
q_composite_answer_p1: Either modular labeling by applying different labels to each component (recommended for long documents), or conservative global label using the most automated label (here AIC) for the entire document.
q_composite_answer_p2: Modular labeling better serves transparency, but choice depends on context.
q_resume_title: I used AI to summarize an article I wrote myself. Which label?
q_resume_answer: Substance (ideas) is human, form (summary) is AI-produced. If you review and validate the summary, HCE label fits. If summary is generated then published with very limited validation, AIC becomes generally the most coherent label.
q_equipe_title: I work in a team: how do I label collective work where some members use AI and others don't?
q_equipe_answer: Label reflects overall process. If team collectively defined substance (ideas, structure) and AI only intervened for final formatting, HCE may fit. If some members delegated substantial parts to AI, choose the label corresponding to highest AI intervention level (principle of caution). You can also note the distribution.
q_audiovideo_title: How do I label audio or video content?
q_audiovideo_intro: The protocol applies identically. For video:
q_audiovideo_ex2: Podcast with cloned voice → AIC (even if script is human)
q_audiovideo_ex3: Real interview with AI subtitles → HCA
q_confiance_title: How can we ensure authors sincerely respect labels?
q_confiance_answer: TCP/UP relies on good faith. There is no built-in technical control to the protocol. However, inconsistent or deceptive usage weakens author credibility and audience trust.
q_scribe_title: Why use the term "Scribe" to describe AI?
q_scribe_answer: This term precisely defines the tool's function in the HCE label context: technical expertise serving intellectual authority. This values the author's design work while acknowledging machine contribution to wording.
q_devalorisation_title: Could using these labels devalue my content?
q_devalorisation_answer: On the contrary. In digital space saturated with anonymous content, transparency becomes a quality seal. Displaying a TCP/UP label demonstrates author assumes their methodology and respects their audience. It is a positive differentiation factor.
q_gratuit_title: Is the protocol free and open to use?
q_gratuit_answer: Yes. TCP/UP is an open standard. It can be freely used by content creators, businesses, or institutions wishing to adopt clear ethical guidelines on AI usage.
q_maj_label_title: What if I discover labeled content doesn't match reality after correction?
q_maj_label_answer: Nothing prevents you from updating the label anytime, for instance during revision. What matters is that labeling is sincere at publication time. If you modify content later, carefully reassess the label.
q_humanwashing_title: Is there a risk of "human‑washing" with this protocol?
q_humanwashing_answer: Risk exists, like any declarative system. TCP/UP is not technical enforcement, its strength relies on usage consistency, author responsibility, and eventually clear public doctrine for handling disputed cases.
cat_perspectives_title: Perspectives and Societal Issues
q_compatcc_title: Is the protocol compatible with other standards (Creative Commons, etc.)?
q_compatcc_answer: Absolutely. TCP/UP adds an informational dimension on production method without interfering with intellectual property or distribution rights issues.
q_evolution_title: How does the protocol plan to adapt to AI evolution?
q_evolution_answer: TCP/UP is designed to be evolutionary. Substance/Form distinctions provide a solid foundation, but protocol can gain additional precision as human-machine interactions refine.
q_crainte_answer: Initial concern may exist, but trend reverses quickly. In digital space saturated with opaquely-generated content, TCP/UP-adopting authors distinguish themselves through integrity. Transparency becomes competitive advantage, transforming general suspicion into declared trust.
q_labelh_title: If AI ever equals human capabilities, will the "H" label retain relevance?
q_labelh_answer: The "H" label does not denote technical performance, but responsibility. A human author is accountable for their ideas, ethics, and writing consequences. AI, as a tool, cannot assume this burden. "H" guarantees readers there is a sovereign responsible person behind content, able to answer for expressed thought.
q_aiact_title: Could this protocol be imposed by future regulation (like AI Act)?
q_aiact_answer: Protocol has no mandate to be legal standard. It can complement voluntary transparency documentation approach, but doesn't alone guarantee regulatory compliance. That always depends on context, content type, and distribution framework.
q_contribuer_title: How can I contribute to protocol evolution?
q_contribuer_answer: TCP/UP is open to feedback. You can propose improvements, report uncovered edge cases, or share experience via the contact page or by opening an issue on the GitHub repository. The project is collaborative by nature.
q_distinctioncf_answer: This distinction is TCP/UP's methodological pillar. In any creation process, one must separate intention (what and why) from execution (how). By isolating Substance from Form, the protocol values human expertise directing reflection while precisely declaring the tool facilitating wording.
q_50pourcent_title: I rewrote about 50% of AI-generated content, which label applies?
q_50pourcent_answer: This is the tipping point between ACE and HCE. If structure and arguments remain mostly from algorithmic proposal, ACE label stands. If your rewriting regained full argumentative control and style, you can opt for HCE. When hesitating, choosing the label crediting AI most is always the most credibility-protective strategy.
q_sectionsdiff_title: How do I handle a document whose sections were produced differently?
q_sectionsdiff_answer: Transparency is information, not rigid constraint. For short documents, apply the most representative label overall. For complex or lengthy documents, you can label each section individually. For example, introduction in HUC and technical summary in HCE.
q_diffhcahce_title: What's the difference between HCA and HCE?
q_diffhcahce_answer: HCA (Human-Centric Assistance) limits to technical corrections without style or substance impact (spelling, grammar, syntax). HCE (Human-Centric Editing) involves AI intervention on form (rephrasing, structuring, tone adaptation) while substance remains human-sourced. If AI modifies substance, move to ACE.
q_souslabels_title: Can I use sub-labels ACE-L, ACE-B, ACE-D in public publications?
q_souslabels_answer: These sub-labels are intended for internal use (technical documentation, metadata). Public displayed badge must remain ACE to avoid reading complexity. You can nonetheless mention these details in a footnote or metadata (data-tcpup-sub).
q_sceptique_declaratif_title: "A declarative system is useless. Anyone can lie."
q_sceptique_declaratif_intro: True. TCP/UP doesn't claim to be infallible. But consider this:
q_sceptique_declaratif_li1: Creative Commons licenses are also declarative. They work.
q_sceptique_declaratif_li2: Label value depends on the reputation of who applies it.
q_sceptique_declaratif_li3: Systematic liars discredit themselves over time.
q_sceptique_declaratif_conclusion: TCP/UP doesn't replace reader vigilance. It gives them a tool to assess author transparency.
q_sceptique_complique_title: "It's too complicated. 5 labels, that's 4 too many."
q_sceptique_complique_p1: Simpler versions were tested (3 labels, 2 labels). They couldn't distinguish spelling correction from co-writing. Current granularity is minimum needed for honest transparency.
q_sceptique_complique_p2: And with the decision tree, choosing takes less than 30 seconds.
q_sceptique_elitiste_answer: No. TCP/UP doesn't rank labels. AIC content (mostly AI-generated) isn't "worse" than HUC content (no AI used). It's different. The objective is information, not judgment.
q_sceptique_confiance_title: "Why should I trust an individual initiative?"
q_sceptique_confiance_intro: You're right to be cautious. That's why:
q_sceptique_confiance_li1: The protocol is open and publicly documented
q_sceptique_confiance_li2: The author applies TCP/UP to their own project (full transparency)
q_sceptique_confiance_li3: A community is forming to ensure governance
q_sceptique_confiance_conclusion: If you don't trust it, don't use TCP/UP. But if you seek a common language for AI transparency, we propose this one.
q_sceptique_aiact_title: "AI Act will make all this mandatory. Why anticipate?"
q_sceptique_aiact_answer: Because AI Act imposes transparency obligations without providing practical method to answer them. TCP/UP proposes concrete documentary approach to meet this transparency spirit today. Organizations adopting the protocol now get clarity on creative processes before legal framework hardens.
q_api_title: Can I integrate TCP/UP badge dynamically via API or script?
q_api_answer: Currently, badges are provided as images (WEBP, SVG) and text signatures. Dynamic version could emerge if demand is strong. For now, you can copy badge HTML from the site's "Kit" section.
q_meta_title: Are <meta name="tcp-up:..."> metadata recognized by Google or search engines?
q_meta_answer: Not yet, but they constitute today a structured declarative trace and can facilitate future uses if more widely recognized standards emerge.
q_badgevsmeta_title: Why favor visual badges over invisible metadata?
q_badgevsmeta_answer: Protocol encourages both uses. However, visual badge serves essential pedagogical function, it directly addresses the reader. Hiding information in metadata would only serve indexing bots, whereas TCP/UP aims to restore immediate trust contract between humans.
q_licences_title: Is the protocol compatible with Creative Commons or open licenses?
q_licences_answer: Absolutely. TCP/UP acts as complementary information layer on production method. Specifying that open-licensed documentation was designed under HCE label is total transparency approach, perfectly aligned with sharing ethics of open-source communities.
q_generateur_title: Is there an automatic HTML code generator to integrate the badge?
q_generateur_answer: Yes, in the "Resources" section you'll find a simple generator letting you choose your label and copy corresponding code (badge image + metadata). The site also offers ready-to-use snippets.
q_reseaux_title: Can I use TCP/UP on platforms not allowing custom code (e.g., social media)?
q_reseaux_answer: In that case, use text signature [TCP/UP: XXX] in caption or content description. This remains human-readable and meets transparency goal, even without visual badge.
q_signalement_title: How do I report abusive label usage?
q_signalement_intro: Currently, TCP/UP has no centralized reporting mechanism. You can:
q_signalement_li2: Report via our contact form (we'll document cases)
q_signalement_conclusion: Eventually, a community registry could enable structured reporting.
